Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to calculate the total duration for each case The process starts with Submit Claim and ends with Close Claim So Ill look at the timestamps for those two activities in each case For Case 2001 Submit 20240401 0900 Close 20240401 1030 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes Case 2002 Submit 20240401 0905 Close 20240402 1100 Duration 1 day 1 hour 55 minutes Case 2003 Submit 20240401 0910 Close 20240403 0930 Duration 2 days 20 minutes Case 2004 Submit 20240401 0920 Close 20240401 1045 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes Case 2005 Submit 20240401 0925 Close 20240404 1430 Duration 3 days 5 hours 5 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Hmm Case 2005 stands out as the longest at over 3 days Cases 2002 and 2003 also take significantly longer than the others The quick ones 2001 and 2004 are under 2 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now looking at the attributes Complexity Low Cases 2001 2004 fast Medium Case 2002 medium duration High Cases 2003 2005 longest durations Thats a clear pattern high complexity claims take much longer

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Region A Cases 2001 2003 mixed durations Region B Cases 2002 2004 2005 mixed durations No strong regional pattern since both regions have fast and slow cases

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Resources CSRJane handles Cases 2001 fast and 2003 slow AdjusterMike handles Cases 2001 fast and 2003 slow AdjusterLisa handles Cases 2002 medium 2004 fast 2005 slow So individual resources arent consistently slow it depends on the case theyre handling

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The real differentiator seems to be complexity High complexity cases 2003 and 2005 have multiple Request Additional Documents events which causes delays Case 2005 has three document requests spanning three days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me verify the document request pattern Case 2003 High 2 requests on same day then approval next day Case 2005 High 3 requests over three days approval on day 4 Whereas mediumlow complexity cases have at most one request or none

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the root cause appears to be 1 High complexity claims inherently require more scrutiny and documentation 2 The process gets stuck in a loop of requesting documents multiple times 3 Each request adds significant delay days in some cases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For mitigation Prescreen high complexity claims upfront to gather all needed docs immediately Set clear documentation requirements before evaluation begins Implement parallel processing where possible Consider automated document verification for standard cases
